en.extract:
The Ethiopian cisticola (Cisticola lugubris) is a species of bird in the family Cisticolidae.
Alternate common names are Abyssinian black-backed cisticola, and winding cisticola (Ethiopian) (eBird).


Taxonomy
The Ethiopian cisticola is monotypic. This taxon was split from the winding cisticola by the IOC and HBW, as were the rufous-winged cisticola, Luapula cisticola and coastal cisticola. The Clements (2017) and Howard and Moore (2014) world lists consider these taxa as a single species, the winding cisticola C. galactotes (sensu lato).


Distribution and habitat
This species is found in the highlands of Ethiopia and Eritrea.
Its natural habitats are tropical seasonally wet or flooded grassland and swamps.


References


External links
 Data related to Cisticola lugubris at Wikispecies

The '''Ethiopian cisticola''' ('''''Cisticola lugubris''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Cisticolidae]].

Alternate [[common name]]s are '''Abyssinian black-backed cisticola''', and '''winding cisticola''' '''(Ethiopian)''' (eBird).<ref name="AVIBASE">{{avibase|3DF3F9B9|Cisticola lugubris}}</ref>

==Taxonomy==
The Ethiopian cisticola is monotypic.<ref name="Gill&Donsker2017">{{cite report|last1=Gill|first1=F.|last2=Donsker|first2=D.|title=IOC World Bird List (v 7.3)|date=2017|doi=10.14344/IOC.ML.7.3|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/|access-date=21 August 2017|doi-access=free}}</ref> This taxon was split from the [[winding cisticola]] by the [[Birds of the World: Recommended English Names|IOC]] and [[HBW and BirdLife International Illustrated Checklist of the Birds of the World|HBW]], as were the [[rufous-winged cisticola]], [[Luapula cisticola]] and [[coastal cisticola]]. The [[The Clements Checklist of Birds of the World|Clements]] (2017) and [[Howard and Moore Complete Checklist of the Birds of the World|Howard and Moore]] (2014) world lists consider these taxa as a single species, the '''winding cisticola ''C. galactotes'' (sensu lato)'''.<ref name="AVIBASE"/>

==Distribution and habitat==
This species is found in the highlands of Ethiopia and Eritrea.<ref name="Ryan2017">{{cite web|last1=Ryan|first1=Peter|title=Ethiopian Cisticola (''Cisticola lugubris'')|url= http://www.hbw.com/node/58623|website=Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive|publisher=Lynx Edicions|access-date=21 August 2017|location=Barcelona|language=en}}</ref>

Its natural [[habitat]]s are tropical seasonally wet or flooded [[grassland]] and [[swamp]]s.
